growth_records.medium: Defined artificial seawater (ESAW, modified) with P. tricornutum exudates + 13C/15N-labeled algal DOM

up.method: nanoSIMS single-cell 13C net incorporation scaled by cell size and abundance
no significant 15N (DON) incorporation vs killed control (Nnet n/a)
medium glycolate incorporator (2-day Cnet 1.5-3%) in separate glycolate/nitrate expt; not a volumetric flux

growth_records.cite: Mayali et al. Single-cell isotope tracing reveals functional guilds of bacteria associated with the diatom Phaeodactylum tricornutum. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-41179-9. [PMC10499878]
growth_records.snippet: *Marinobacter 3-2 ... Nnet n/a, Cnet CD 19%, µg C from DOC 1.8, µg N from DON 0.0 (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 volumetric net uptake: DOC 1.8 µg C L-1 d-1; DON 0.0 (no significant complex DON incorporation). Abundance 2.01E6 cells/mL. Cnet CD 19%. Mutualist, increases algal lipid/biomass; consumes glycolate and glucose (GC-MS). Small-molecule user. No µ.

growth_records.medium: Hot Lake Heterotrophic (HLH) medium + 5 mM glucose + 1x Wolfe's vitamins

up.method: sole carbon source 5 mM; specific rate not quantified

sec.method: NMR at mid-log 20 h; Marinobacter HL-58 secretes ethanol on glucose (down 23% in co-culture)

growth_records.cite: Khan, Nymul et al. Phenotypic responses to interspecies competition and commensalism in a naturally-derived microbial co-culture. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-18630-1. [PMC5762899]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from log-linear regression); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.215=3.224 h
growth_records.notes: Axenic monoculture of prokaryote Marinobacter sp. HL-58 on 5 mM glucose. mu_max=0.215 h-1 reported (Fig.1C). td=ln2/0.215=3.224 h. Ethanol secretion detected by NMR (Fig.1E). No mmol/gDW/h rates or biomass yields in text; qS not derivable.

growth_records.medium: HLH medium + 5 mM glucose (co-culture with Halomonas HL-48)

up.method: shared glucose; outcompeted; rate not quantified

sec.method: NMR mid-log 20 h; ethanol ~23% lower vs Marinobacter axenic (p=0.101)

growth_records.cite: Khan, Nymul et al. Phenotypic responses to interspecies competition and commensalism in a naturally-derived microbial co-culture. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-18630-1. [PMC5762899]
growth_records.method: calculated from reported '20% decrease' vs axenic: 0.215 x 0.80 = 0.172 h-1; td=ln2/0.172=4.030 h
growth_records.snippet: Marinobacter HL-58 was severely affected by competition and decreased its specific growth rate by 20% from its axenic performance.
growth_records.notes: Marinobacter HL-58 outcompeted in glucose co-culture. mu calculated from stated -20% vs axenic 0.215: 0.215*0.80=0.172 h-1. td=ln2/0.172=4.030 h. Ethanol down ~23% (p=0.101, n.s.). Absolute values in figure only.

up.method: no growth; cannot catabolize xylose (no xylFGH / xylose isomerase in genome)

growth_records.cite: Khan, Nymul et al. Phenotypic responses to interspecies competition and commensalism in a naturally-derived microbial co-culture. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-18630-1. [PMC5762899]
growth_records.method: reported: no growth observed (Marinobacter HL-58 lacks xylose transporters and xylose isomerase)
growth_records.snippet: the Marinobacter HL-58 axenic control on the xylose minimal medium, where no growth could be observed
growth_records.notes: Negative-growth condition: Marinobacter HL-58 does not grow on xylose as sole carbon source (mu=0), genetically explained by absence of xylose uptake/isomerase genes. Useful negative datapoint for model constraints.

growth_records.medium: Brackish carbonate-buffered sulfate-reducer medium (Widdel & Bak 1992, without sulfate) + North Sea crude oil

up.method: growth yield estimated assuming alkane utilization; specific rate not reported

yields.note: assuming Marinobacter responsible for all alkane degradation; low yield suggests minor role

growth_records.cite: Gray et al. The quantitative significance of Syntrophaceae and syntrophic partnerships in methanogenic degradation of crude oil alkanes. Environmental Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.1111/j.1462-2920.2011.02570.x. [PMC3258425]
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rate (mu) calculated for Marinobacter sp. in the amended microcosms was similar to that determined for Smithella/Syntrophus (0.019 day-1; doubling time 36 days)... estimated growth yield for Marinobacter was only 0.0005
growth_records.notes: Environmental microcosm, mixed community. mu = 0.019 day-1 (exponential qPCR fit R2=0.61); = 0.019/24 = 7.9e-4 /h; doubling time 36 days = 864 h. Growth yield 0.0005 g cell-C/g alkane-C reported. Authors note low yield implies minor role in alkane degradation. Temperature/pH not stated.
